fields.narrative_intro: *A smug person and an overconfident person discuss Apple's Gemini deal in a submarine running out of oxygen.*
fields.panel1:
[setup]
SMUG PERSON: "Apple Intelligence is finally good."
OVERCONFIDENT PERSON: "Told you. Pure Apple innovation."
fields.panel2:
[build]
SMUG PERSON: "Deep collaboration. Huge upgrade. Their words."
OVERCONFIDENT PERSON: "Classic Cupertino engineering mastery."
fields.panel3:
[pivot]
SMUG PERSON: "It's Gemini. Google built the models."
OVERCONFIDENT PERSON: "...Private Cloud Compute though."
fields.panel4:
[silent reaction]
[Visual: The overconfident person stares at a Think Different poster duct-taped to the submarine wall. A slow oxygen bubble drifts past. Neither person blinks.]
fields.panel5:
[punch]
OVERCONFIDENT PERSON: "So Apple's privacy pitch is... Google?"
SMUG PERSON: "Google never sees your data. Apple just uses it."
fields.panel6:
[button]
[Visual: The submarine's Siri speaker quietly plays 'Don't Be Evil' on repeat. Nobody addresses it.]

narrative_summary: The joke lands on Apple's decade-long privacy-vs-Google posturing evaporating the moment Gemini powers their flagship AI feature, delivered deadpan as the oxygen runs out.
rationale: Candidate 1 earns its score by landing a genuinely sharp closing line — 'Google never sees your data. Apple just uses it.' — that reframes the privacy contradiction with precision rather than restating it, and the 'Don't Be Evil' Siri button is a specific, earned callback that most writers would have left on the table. The deadpan voice also does real work here: the submarine oxygen conceit stays atmospheric rather than becoming the joke itself.
story_title: Apple reveals new AI architecture built around Google Gemini models
place: in a submarine running out of oxygen
